body: Do not forget to add BREAKING CHANGE: .. in the commit message.
We are enhancing the data passed to the template. In order to easily identify what are the magic variable like url from the one from the user, I chose to prefix them with +.

> I chose to prefix them with +.

That's an exotic choice (= we do not see it often) but still interesting I find.

> We are enhancing the data passed to the template. In order to easily identify what are the magic variable like url from the one from the user, I chose to prefix them with +.

Hmmmm, maybe `_` is more appropriated then? And we need at least to do the same with the helpers.

The problems with `_` are that it : 
- usually denotes private (this is not private)
- could be used in user data (we would love to avoid conflicts)

It's exotic on purpose, and for widget implementer / deep customizer (who will copy paste the code from the example).

> The problems with _ are that it :
> 
> usually denotes private (this is not private)
> could be used in user data (we would love to avoid conflicts)
> It's exotic on purpose, and for widget implementer / deep customizer (who will copy paste the code from the example).

I agree but I would definitely not introduce any "new" syntax/convention.

> could be used in user data (we would love to avoid conflicts)

Yes _url will be used in user data that's for sure.

Otherwise we could just have an `_ais: {helpers, refinementUrl}` and we would be good in most situations

> I agree but I would definitely not introduce any "new" syntax/convention.

I would just nest everything in something convenient like `{_.helpers.MYHELPER}` or `{_.url}`

> About refinementUrl, what do you think? More precise than url which is generic.

Hmm `refinementUrl` is not really compliant with the pagination widget or any other non-refinementList widget (like share url, etc...).

The way I've implemented how the data are passed to the template, let you pass any kind of contextual data. Maybe it's too much and we should only pass the url. But then I think this is not so isolated as a use case, and I would generally prefer computed data instead of functions like in helpers. Also for function template it's another parameter that is passed to the template function. What do you think?

Also `_ais` is ok but it's not very meaningful compared to `helpers` which is already added in the user data in the case of hogan templates.

> Also _ais is ok but it's not very meaningful compared to helpers which is already added in the user data in the case of hogan templates

Would go for `_ais: {helpers, refinementUrl, ...}` Helpers are only functions, otherwise we can have properties. Or we go `_ais: {fn1, fn2, refinementUrl}` but then you do not know what's a fn (not data bound), what's a property (data bound) you can use?
Oh maybe we should just add that `url` to the current `helpers` namespace, without any `_` or `_ais`?

As for the CSS conflicts, _ais is the safest and explicit way to indicate "This belongs to algolia instant search".

Using `{{_ais.helpers}}` and `{{data._ais.url}}` (or the property name we will find) is not something hard I feel.

Having helpers in subnamespaces helps identifying what if a fn you can use, what is only a property.

When debugging etc you will never ask yourself where does this comes from. Given that you know what is "_ais". But at some point you read the documentation about helpers, transformData or url then you know what is it.

The thing is, `_ais` looks like it's private and we don't want users to use it. But it actually contains `helpers` that are here so the users use them.

For this specific use-case, I would put `url` inside `helpers`.

> For this specific use-case, I would put url inside helpers.

But then how do you make the distinction between functions (like what we have right now in helpers) and values?

(from a discussion with @pixelastic) Ok so let's close the prefix problem right away. There is no need for the template to be able to handle contextual data (with prefix, namespace or whatever) because the only case we pass more data (here the url), is when we don't have user data but our data. Let's just add the url parameter directly in the data passed to the template and remove any custom code from it to handle contextual data.

Agreed?

> Agreed?

WFM
Strangely this PR is not building on travis, it's always "waiting"
Youll have to reopen in another branch I think, it's kind of lost in the middle of the internet. But let's review first here otherwise we lost all comments
